text: The surgeon performs a fully stapled intracorporeal anastomosis, placing stay sutures resembling atlas clamps to hold the common aneurotomy open before stapling it closed. Three silk interrupted sutures are used in the process.

He takes two loads to come across this common aneurotomy. and does an elegant and beautiful job in doing so. Now, I've done it like this several times before, and just like he does here, he's over-sewing it with a couple of limber sutures. I've done it like this, and my personal preference is not to do the stapled anastomosis only because I end up taking more time to close this aneurotomy with a stapled technique. then it would take me to close it with a suture technique. And secondly, when you do staple across the common aneuronum, you do lose a little bit of luminal circumference, and therefore I do suggest you use probably two fires down the common channel.
